title: Feature request: Ability to move full screen RDP session between monitors
author: DavidRobinsonDk
body_snippet:
Ability to move full screen RDP session between monitors, including the ability to have multiple full screen RDP sessions open at the same time.

## Expected Behavior
With mstsc.exe I can "restore" the full screen session to a window, move the window to another monitor and go back to full screen.

## Current Behavior
Currently I can see no way to do this in mRemoteNG.
If I "restore" a full screen RDP session in mRemoteNG, it gets put in in tab.
A tabbed window goes to full screen on the ...

comments.author: ajw625
comments.snippet: I would really like to have this feature. Every day I turn off my monitors and the RDP windows move to my main monitor. I have to disconnect, move mRemoteNG to the second monitor, and reconnect, which is a bit slow. Sometimes there is a lag for 30 seconds or so before it is usable.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `0d23eec0` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Feature request: Ability to move full screen RDP session bet

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage (claude): Valid UX enhancement with clear user demand: full-screen RDP sessions should support monitor switching without reconnect, leveraging MSTSCLib's existing multi-monitor/ContainerHandledFullScreen or sending the RDP window to a different screen.
Approach: Expose a 'Move to monitor' action for active full-screen RDP tabs by calling MSTSCLib's ContainerHandledFullScreen or programmatically repositioning the ActiveX host window to the target screen bounds, then toggling full-screen off/on to re-enter on the new monitor.
